headlines.title: UAE Oil Output Hits All-Time High, Doubling Pre-Crisis Levels
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) produced 4.1 million barrels per day (bpd) of crude oil in June, its highest output ever, according to estimates by the International Energy Agency. The UAE’s crude oil production jumped from 3.3 million bpd in May to 4.1 million bpd in June after t

headlines.title: Bureaucracy Is Strangling America's Clean Energy Buildout
headlines.summary: The global energy system is facing a polycrisis. headlines.title: IEA Cuts Russia's Oil Production Forecast Due to Ukrainian Attacks
headlines.summary: Russia's oil production in 2026 and 2027 is set to be lower than previously expected, due to intensified Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia's energy infrastructure, the International Energy Agency said in its monthly oil market report on Friday. Russia produced on average 9.2 mill

headlines.title: Oil Prices Rally on Renewed Hormuz Supply Risks
headlines.summary: August WTI crude oil traded higher during the week ending July 10, with prices last at $71.84 through Thursday evening, up $3.38, or 4.94%. The contract posted a wide weekly range, climbing from a low of $67.82 to a high of $76.08 before pulling back late Thursday. The week's adv

headlines.title: ADNOC Orders $900 Million in New LNG Carriers to Expand Global Fleet
headlines.summary: ADNOC Logistics and Services has placed a $900-million order for four newbuild LNG carriers to expand its fleet as Abu Dhabi’s national oil company seeks to boost gas exports to seize the global rise in LNG demand. headlines.title: IEA Warns Renewed U.S.-Iran Conflict Could Upend Oil Surplus Forecast
headlines.summary: Despite the tentative recovery of oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz and the first build-up in global stocks since the war began, this week’s re-escalation of the U.S.-Iran hostilities could flip the outlook for an oil market surplus for next year, the International Energy Ag

headlines.title: Fuel Refining Margins Hit Record Highs as Markets Tighten
headlines.summary: Refining margins for gasoline and diesel jumped this week to new record highs after the re-escalation in the Middle East, Russia’s ban on diesel exports, and crumbling global fuel inventories. The surge in fuel margins and the price spread over crude prices suggest that the globa

headlines.title: More LNG Carriers Brave the Strait of Hormuz Despite Renewed Hostilities
headlines.summary: Close to half a dozen liquefied natural gas carriers have gone into the Strait of Hormuz in the past few days and six have exited the strait, despite the recent return to hostilities between the United States and Iran. According to Kpler data cited by Reuters, five ballast LNG ta

headlines.title: India Expands Strategic Oil Reserves With New ONGC Storage Plan
headlines.summary: India’s ONGC plans to build up a strategic national reserve of crude totalling 1.75 million tons, or 12.8 million barrels.
